Patch Set 4:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

while I am OK with patch in general it doesn't look as big win for me. Now LU_KEY_INIT() uses kmalloc() to allocate session and thread info, which in turn also works via slab allocation, so are there other benefits except better memory usage? In ticket you've mentioned that vmalloc is avoided, could you elaborate?

Patch Set 4:

any news with review ?

Patch Set 4:

Mike, large page list will turn an allocations to the vmalloc, tgt keys is not a power 2 sizes, so large fragmentation and non optimal usage. It help with crash dump analyze as keys may found with a kmem command.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

So I read the ticket and commit description and I am not sure what's up with this patch other than we can actually track allocations for this bit separately.

Do we really allocate so many of these things all the time, I think it was one per service thread = relatively little? (also would mean we have number of threads of allocations here).

How is the allocation improved? The ticket also emntions vmalloc pressure - is the structure bigger than 4k? You do realize currently we always try regular malloc first before proceeding to do vmalloc on failure, right?
And if regular malloc failed - assuming this is the path here, then we'd just have allocation failure instead of a vmalloc attempt which does not look like any improvement in behavior.

I feel like better explanation is needed. If any profiling was performed to show additional benefits, please show it as well.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

actually -1 until the information is available and to drop this patch off from my radar.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Ok, I discussed this a bit with Shadow.

The main benefits are less memory fragmentation because of lack of rounding up to the next power of 2 and it also makes things easier to debug in crash since it's easy to find the sessions. I guess the benefits are there to allow this.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 1755c9f628c2a9f240c1d78765c4d4dd74c46e99 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-10101 tests: correct sanity-quota call to quota_error

commit.subject: LU-8475 target: use slab allocation
commit.message:
LU-8475 target: use slab allocation

The patch adds kmem slabs for target threads and session info
to improve allocation and better accounting.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander.boyko@seagate.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey.lyashkov@seagate.com>

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2836

Tested-by: Jenkins
